title: anacamptis morio subsp. longicornu
intro: Anacamptis morio subsp. longicornu, also known as the northern marsh orchid, is a rare and unique subspecies of the early-purple orchid, which is a type of flowering plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome due to its high concentration of alkaloids. The health benefits of Anacamptis morio subsp. longicornu include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which may contribute to its ability to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, and it is typically dried and made into teas or infusions, or used in combination with other herbs to create tinctures and tonics.
